message:
fix(ci): match rust-toolchain.toml channel in dist.yml

franken_whisper pins to nightly via rust-toolchain.toml at the repo
root, so installing `@stable` via dtolnay/rust-toolchain at the top
of the workflow had no effect on the build (cargo overrides to nightly
per the toolchain file) and added a wasted toolchain fetch. Worse, the
build job's `targets: \${{ matrix.target }}` parameter was attached to
the stable toolchain rather than the nightly one cargo actually uses,
forcing a second on-demand target download for each matrix entry.

Switch both jobs to `@nightly` so the channel — and the cross-compile
target — match what the build will actually run with.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,7 +15,10 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v6
 
-      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      # franken_whisper pins to nightly via rust-toolchain.toml; install
+      # the nightly channel up front so cargo doesn't have to fetch it on
+      # demand (and so we get rustfmt + clippy on the same channel).
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
         with:
           components: rustfmt, clippy
 
@@ -74,8 +77,11 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v6
 
-      - name: Install Rust stable
-        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      # franken_whisper pins nightly via rust-toolchain.toml; matching that
+      # here lets the cross-compile target install live on the same channel
+      # cargo will actually use for the build.
+      - name: Install Rust nightly with target
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
         with:
           targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
